text: Out-of-sample confirmation + extension of the two-term mechanism (a-0020). On gen_7x7_s3_00 — a second tier-3 puzzle the cost model was never built on (search_nodes 3789 vs the 4547 it used) — the discount-scaled two-term net (net x 0.335 from e-0017) predicts the actual billed none-vs-coarse gap on all 4 rungs in sign AND rough magnitude: tier-1 +0.27 vs +0.33, tier-2 +2.23 vs +2.16, tier-3a -7.85 vs -10.85, tier-3b +0.09 vs +0.01. The model thus generalizes across a +$2.2 -> -$10.9 range, INCLUDING the regime where coarse tools actively hurt (coarse cache_read explodes to 15.4M tok, tax beats saving). Refinement of a-0013: the Goldilocks 'benefit' is not single-signed at the hard end — a near-identically graded tier-3 puzzle flips it strongly negative. Caveat: tier-3a is n=1 per cell.
